score_run.geography: TX — DFW + Houston + San Antonio + Austin metros, target 60-90 verified independents
score_run.notes: TX has no statewide professional licensing board for collision repair (TX DMV salvage license is per-vehicle; I-CAR Gold Class is voluntary). Spine assembled from BBB/Carwise/Google/Yelp/Autobody News + per-shop website verification. Heavy PE/MSO/franchise/dealership exclusion enforced.

businesses.owner_name: Founder family (unverified specific name; family-owned per company About page)

signals.evidence: Founded 1987 per company About page; BBB A+ rating since 1987 corroborates; 39 yrs in business as of 2026.
Dallas metro is the #1 TX target market for collision MSOs (Caliber, Crash Champions, Classic Collision, QCG all actively acquiring in DFW). Hail corridor = premium pricing.

businesses.layer1_comment: Founder family of Texas Body Works has operated since 1987 (39 yrs personal tenure). Specific owner name not publicly verified — owner_age estimated at ~62 using a license_tenure_proxy (a founder who personally launched in 1987 at typical founding age 25-35 would now be ~62-72). Confidence is medium-low without OV65 verification; this is the natural-exit window if the original founder is still owner.
businesses.layer2_comment: 39 yrs in business, BBB A+ since 1987, multi-tech facility on Reeder Rd Dallas, clean record. Recurring revenue via insurance DRP relationships (multi-carrier per website). Estimated ~$1.5-2.5M revenue — solid SBA 7(a) financeable size.
businesses.layer3_comment: Website is modern and maintained, online estimate form present — partial coasting evidence. No publicly listed associate or successor info on website; no live team-page fetch completed this run. Cannot promote past B without verified successor check + verified owner age.
businesses.layer4_comment: Dallas metro is the most heavily-rolled-up collision market in TX. Caliber, Crash Champions, Classic Collision, QCG (Susquehanna PE) all actively acquiring. Hail corridor = high recurring auto-body demand. Premium ZIP context (75229 is north Dallas, near affluent catchments).
businesses.final_comment: Long-tenured (39-yr) independent Dallas family shop, BBB A+ since founding 1987. Owner age and successor status are not directly verified from public sources — flagging as B until live team-page fetch and CAD OV65 lookup can be done. High Layer 4 (Dallas = top consolidation metro). Forward to ETA / search-fund community.
businesses.value_add_thesis: Insurance DRP roster expansion (add State Farm Select Service if not already, USAA Auto Center, GEICO Xpress), ADAS calibration in-house buildout (currently not advertised), modern customer-portal SMS status updates, automated review-gen, and an associate-to-owner transition path. Likely 1.3-1.7x EBITDA path over 24-36 months.

businesses.owner_name: Walker family (2nd generation; specific principal name not publicly listed)

signals.evidence: Website explicitly states '2nd generation' — successor is already in place, the family-internal transition has occurred or is in progress. This reduces L1 'natural exit' base rate substantially.
Founded 1981, 45 yrs in business; family-run continuously per website.

businesses.layer1_comment: 2nd-generation family-run shop. Successor is already in the business — current operator is likely 45-60 yrs old (took over from founder generation in 1990s-2000s). Not a natural-exit-window owner; their internal exit path is to the next generation, not an outside buyer.
businesses.layer2_comment: 45 yrs in business, multi-service (collision, frame, paint, PDR), South Dallas location. Smaller crew estimate (~8). Solid SBA-financeable size at ~$700K-$1.2M revenue.
businesses.layer3_comment: Website is modern but not dated. Multi-generation family means active operating posture continues. No strong coasting tells; current operator is mid-career.
businesses.layer4_comment: Dallas market = top TX consolidation target. Mid-sized independent family shop is exactly the QCG/Caliber profile.
businesses.final_comment: 2nd-generation Walker family shop, founded 1981. Already passed through one succession — current operator is mid-career, not in natural exit window. C_watch: re-evaluate in 90+ days if succession dynamics change. Solid business, but not currently a coasting-pre-sale profile.
businesses.value_add_thesis: Not currently pursuable — successor already internal. If/when 2nd-gen owner approaches retirement (likely 2030s), revisit. Not actionable now.

businesses.final_comment: Raven Auto Body Round Rock — 12-yr 2-owner shop, Mazda OEM. Co-owner-internal exit path more likely. C_watch.
